_meta.description: Verified government and opposition terms for state and union-territory context. This ledger is separate from the adverse-event record and grows jurisdiction by jurisdiction.
_meta.sourcePolicy: Term-level citations must resolve to URL-backed entries in data/sources.json; official government, legislature, election, court, or other primary records are preferred.

governmentTerms.leader: No separate Ladakh Union Territory administration
governmentTerms.coalition: Ladakh formed part of the former State of Jammu and Kashmir
governmentTerms.basis: The Union Territory did not exist before 31 October 2019. This interval is recorded to prevent a fabricated pre-2019 Ladakh government; the former Jammu and Kashmir state-government chronology belongs in that jurisdiction's ledger.

Ladakh came into existence without a legislature on 31 October 2019, when Mathur was sworn in as its first Lieutenant Governor. The successor's official oath record places the next assumption on 19 February 2023.

Gupta took the oath on 18 July 2025 and was succeeded when Vinai Kumar Saxena signed the charge certificate and took oath on 13 March 2026.
Saxena took oath and signed the Presidential charge certificate on 13 March 2026; the current Ladakh administration continues to list him in office.

oppositionTerms.office: Ladakh territorial Leader of the Opposition

oppositionTerms.status: Not applicable: Union Territory without a legislature

oppositionTerms.confidence: High holder; exact formal recognition and cessation dates unknown

oppositionTerms.fromLabel: BJP legislators selected Adhikari on 10 May 2021; exact Speaker-recognition date not verified
oppositionTerms.basis: BJP won 77 seats and its legislature party selected Adhikari on 10 May. Assembly records establish that he held the LoP office in the Seventeenth Assembly, which ended when that Assembly was dissolved effective 7 May 2026. The party-selection date is recorded without inventing the exact date of the Speaker's formal recognition.
oppositionTerms.confidence: High holder and endpoint; exact formal recognition start unknown

oppositionTerms.status: New-Assembly transition before any operative recognition
oppositionTerms.basis: AITC was the largest opposition party, but the new House had not yet produced an operative LoP recognition.

oppositionTerms.status: Disputed and provisional: Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay claimed oral recognition, but no settled written certification was located
oppositionTerms.basis: AITC initially nominated Chattopadhyay and he performed the customary escort after the Speaker's election. The later High Court record shows a signature dispute, further verification and no safe basis to present this interval as a completed, uncontested statutory tenure.
oppositionTerms.confidence: High that status was disputed; low as a settled formal tenure

oppositionTerms.party: Elected on an All India Trinamool Congress ticket; party-membership dispute pending
oppositionTerms.basis: The Speaker issued a written recognition order after 58 of the 80 AITC-elected MLAs supported Banerjee's request and 56 appeared personally. The High Court refused interim relief on 18 June; a Division Bench reserved its order on the interim appeal on 17 July. No superseding decision was located by the 22 July cutoff, and a 3 July official parliamentary release identifies him as LoP.
oppositionTerms.confidence: High that the recognition was operative at the cutoff; merits and interim appeal pending
